description_long: This is a medieval-style helmet crafted from dark metal, featuring a fully enclosed faceplate with a pronounced visor and riveted side details. The most striking feature is a large pair of naturalistic antlers mounted on either side of the helmet, curving upward and outward, giving it a dramatic and imposing appearance. The helmet's surface is matte and slightly rough, with no visible decorative graphics or emblems. The antlers appear to be made from real or imitation bone, contrasting with the metallic body. There are no visible ventilation holes or retention straps on the exterior. The interior is likely hollow to accommodate a human head, and the helmet is designed for ornamental or ceremonial use rather than modern protection.
description: A dark metal medieval helmet with large, realistic antlers mounted on top, featuring a matte finish and riveted details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the helmet's pronounced visor, riveted side, and the full curvature of the antlers.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the helmet's central ridge and the symmetrical spread of the antlers.
description_view_2: Angled side view emphasizing the helmet's faceplate and the branching structure of the antlers.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the open faceplate and the dramatic, forward-reaching antlers.
